incidents.title: Resident Fall in Room 204
incidents.description: Resident experienced a fall while getting out of bed

questions.questionText: What time did the incident occur?

answer.answerText: The fall occurred at approximately 8:15 AM when I was doing morning rounds.
Minor bruising on the left hip. Nurse assessed and applied ice pack.

questions.questionText: How did you discover the dietary error?

answer.answerText: I discovered it before noon on friday

incidents.title: Wandering Resident - Exit Attempt
incidents.description: Resident with dementia attempted to leave through emergency exit

questions.questionText: What security measures were in place?

answer.answerText: Door alarm was active. I was able to redirect the resident before he reached the door. He was looking for his wife.

incidents.title: Aggressive Behavior Towards Staff
incidents.description: Resident became verbally aggressive during medication administration

incidents.title: Skin Tear - Left Forearm
incidents.description: Small skin tear discovered during morning care

answer.answerText: Yes, daughter was called immediately. She was understanding and thanked us for the prompt care.

incidents.summary: Minor skin tear treated with proper wound care protocol. Family notified and satisfied with response.

humanReport.summary: During morning care, discovered a small skin tear on Dorothy Wilson's left forearm. Wound was cleaned per protocol, antibiotic ointment applied, and covered with non-adherent dressing. Daughter was notified immediately and expressed gratitude for prompt care.
humanReport.insights: Skin tear likely occurred during sleep due to fragile skin. No signs of trauma or external cause. Resident takes blood thinners which increases skin fragility.
humanReport.recommendations: Continue monitoring skin integrity during all care activities. Consider long-sleeve protection garments at night. Review blood thinner dosage with physician if skin tears become frequent.
humanReport.actions: Daily dressing changes for 7 days. Photograph healing progress. Family follow-up call scheduled for end of week.

aiReport.summary: Minor skin tear incident on left forearm of 82-year-old resident during morning care routine. Proper wound care protocol followed immediately. Family notification completed with positive response.
aiReport.insights:
1. What happened? Small skin tear discovered during routine morning care
2. What happened to the resident? Minor injury with proper immediate treatment, no complications
3. How could we have prevented this? Increased monitoring of skin integrity for residents on anticoagulants, protective garments
4. What should we do to prevent future incidents? Implement proactive skin protection protocols for high-risk residents
aiReport.recommendations:
1. Implement protective garment protocol for residents with fragile skin
2. Staff training on gentle handling techniques for residents on blood thinners
3. Daily skin integrity assessments during care routines
4. Consider consult with wound care specialist for prevention strategies
aiReport.actions:
1. Nursing Director: Review and update skin care protocols (within 1 week)
2. CNA Team: Attend skin integrity training session (within 2 weeks)
3. Charge Nurse: Implement daily skin checks for high-risk residents (immediate)
4. Facility Manager: Source appropriate protective garments (within 1 week)
